steps.title: Use the rebound prediction
steps.content: If REM sleep is homeostatically regulated, seals deprived of REM sleep in the water should compensate after returning to land. The graph would need to show a consistent, significant increase above baseline.

steps.title: Reject mismatched evidence
steps.content: A compares the first two days without addressing baseline compensation. B concerns baseline levels, but the graph reports percentages of baseline. D compares land sleep with in-water sleep, which the graph does not show.

steps.title: Match the researchers' claim
steps.content: The seals do not consistently show a significant REM rebound after deprivation, which supports the researchers' conclusion. The answer is C.
